title: Hilton, Becerra lead in California’s governor primary. See live results here.
description: Latest California governor primary results as Democrats Katie Porter, Tom Steyer and Xavier Becerra and Republicans Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton vie for nominations to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom.

title: Former Biden official Deb Haaland wins New Mexico primary for governor
description: Haaland could make history as the first female Native American governor if she’s elected.

title: Shower of boos expected when Trump becomes first sitting president to attend NBA Finals
description: Some Knicks fans say the president is not welcome at Madison Square Garden.

title: The red state, blue state divide is real. But it's driven by more than just politics
description: Recent research suggests there's more going on with "ideological sorting" than simply moving to places that match one's politics. It's often one of many deciding factors, such as taxes or safety.

title: Political scandals once ended careers. Are they as powerful as they once were?
description: There was a time when scandals were the death knell for political careers. But today, they're far from being career enders. Do scandals really not hold any power anymore?

title: Trump walks out of interview after being pressed on election fraud claims
description: President Trump walked out of an interview on Sunday's "Meet the Press" after being pressed on his repeated claims that the 2020 election and last week's California primaries were "rigged."

title: Israel launches strikes on Iran after Tehran fires barrage of missiles
description: The exchange, after Tehran’s first such strike on Israel in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

title: Trump rejects idea that Iran betrays his 'no new wars' campaign message
description: President Trump is dismissing the idea that launching the war with Iran betrayed his refrain of "No new wars" as he campaigned for the White House in 2024.

title: Trump walks out of ‘Meet the Press’ interview when challenged over false claims
description: When pressed by host Kristen Welker, the president cited no evidence for claims about Jan. 6 and elections he said were “rigged.”

title: A big win for Trump's deportation agenda, with another fight looming
description: The Senate passed a major immigration enforcement funding bill. Now, the measure goes to the House. Lawmakers must also decide the future of a surveillance program set to expire next week.

title: On X, influencers are using prediction markets to sow distrust about election results
description: On X, posts about prediction markets are becoming the latest way for influencers to sow doubt about election results.

title: Politics chat: Maine to vote on Platner, California waits primary results
description: An upcoming primary election in Maine and last week's primary elections in California mean campaign season is well underway.

title: U.S. attorney’s office in California announces probe into elections
description: The Los Angeles U.S. attorney's office said it has launched an investigation into election fraud in California's elections.

title: Trump pardons former GOP congressman convicted of insider trading
description: President Donald Trump issued a full pardon for Steve Buyer, a former Indiana Republican congressman who was convicted of insider trading in 2023.

title: Top Trump artificial intelligence adviser to leave the White House
description: Sriram Krishnan, who has been central to the administration’s AI efforts, will probably continue to play an active role in its approach to the technology.

title: Who will face Karen Bass? LA voters still waiting to find out
description: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.

title: Pope Leo says war with Iran is not a 'just war'
description: Pope Leo XIV said the war in Iran does not qualify as a "just war" according to Catholic teaching, while answering questions by journalists aboard the papal plane for his six-day visit to Spain.

title: The Supreme Court races to decide several explosive cases before its recess
description: The next few weeks will bring decisions in several major Supreme Court cases from birthright citizenship and immigration to the president's power to fire federal officials.
